reviews.review_description:
absolutely love this game. I've had it for a while and I still play it a few times a week.

 I beat all the boards and I probably gone back through them several times. 

I don't really care for the infinity play. Because if you lose around you got to start over from the beginning. 

I wish they would just create more regular levels to work through. But I'm still enjoying playing the levels that I've already played.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the awesome review! You made my day:) Love hearing that you enjoy the levels and the game as a whole! I agree infinity play isn't thought through very well. 

The game was not updated for some time, but it's about time!
Hope we can deliver on some new levels in comming months.

reviews.review_description: So much fun, love it, problem i played for 5 hours and all levels are completed, is there more coming?

reviews.review_title: Amazing hand tracking makes it worth it
reviews.review_description: It's kind of easy and same tacticks work most of levels, but it feels really awsome to play. One of the few VR games that got me really hooked. Hand tracking is awsome and makes the game really standout!

reviews.review_title: Early game is a bit slow
reviews.review_description:
For whatever reason the slow pace of early game kinda stressed me out. There's a button to speed up levels. That will help a lot. My one complaint is more about the level by level we unlock our toys. Feels like another game, that had better level design, no offense. 

Unlocking the toys one by one doesn't feel great. I understand from a design perspective but from player experience it was more annoying than rewarding. 

I'd like the ability to unlock everything right away. Play levels in whatever order I choose.

Gameplay suggestions, environment effects.
Explain the stun mechanic better. Having plots that become affected by damage, weather, enemies. Enemy's could build a tower that spawns reinforcements, weather could leave puddles or mud and zombies could slow down or have different animations based on the weather. 

Enemies could make use of the weather for special enemies. New toys could affect the field and debuff zombies like support towers. Apologies as I'm still in the early game and have not experienced 50% of what's to offer. These were just suggestions I had while I was playing.

Pants vs zombies in vr
Works really well and had a blast playing it. It's a bit short but that's what I've come to expect from a VR game. Definitely recommend. It goes on sale a lot so take an advantage of that.

This is one of the most addictive tower defence games I've played on any platform. It's basically a VR version of Plants vs. Zombies, and it's every bit as awesome as it sounds.

You collect stars (the game's currency) to build an army of toys that you use to defend your castle from the monsters that are trying to invade it. Each toy requires varying amounts of stars to "purchase", which adds an extra strategic element to the game. Do you spend your stars on the cheaper candles, or do you save up to acquire a fire-breathing dragon or a tank?

Each toy is unique, with its own special abilities that can be used to slow down or destroy monsters. At first it seems easy, but by the end of the game you'll be frantically collecting stars, deploying units and sabotaging the efforts of the invading monsters.

Great game, and one of the best mixed reality games I've played on the Meta Quest. My only wish is that there were a few more levels. If the developer were to release DLCs for additional levels I would buy them in a heartbeat.

Don't sleep on this real hidden gem.

reviews.review_title: What a monster of a game!
reviews.review_description:
Awesome!
This is like Plants v Zombies, but in VR… and with Toys…. And monsters!

Simple yet addictive game play… stop the monsters from reaching your doors on the left. Each wave getting harder and harder!
Use the toys you unlock through gameplay, to block, shoot, explode the oncoming monsters!
Plenty of enemy variation throughout the game.

So many neat little touches to this game. Can pick up the monster helmets, sweep them off the floor, flick and touch plants and trees, open n close the tiny chests, prod the evil guy and he gives you a surprise lol

It’s cool how it introduces each new unlock… gives you a gift, pop it open, shows you on the small screen what the new unlock does, and gives you a card that you can hold and look at.

Sounds and music is perfect for this game.

I recommend this so much if you like puzzle games with the “just one more go” feel :)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for playing Toy Monsters and dropping us a review! 🙏

reviews.review_title: I didn’t like it…I LOVED it!
reviews.review_description:
Just started playing this it’s soooo addictive and fun! Wish I didn’t have to go to work today so I can keep playing 

Is very similar to plants vs zombies but way more interactive which I adore! 

I’m glad I bought this
Fun game especially if you like pvz
This game is fantastic! Try it out, you'll love it!!
It's fun, but much shorter than I expected.

reviews.review_title: It's obvious what it's trying to be...
reviews.review_description: and it does an incredible job of it. If you're a fan of PvZ, you will love this game. It does a near perfect job of integrating MR and hand tracking.
I finished this game in 2 days. Wish there would have been more challenges.

developer_response.body: Hi there, we're working on more; keep an eye out for future updates, and don't forget to check out the endless mode on each of the maps!

reviews.review_description:
A really good start. I enjoyed what there is so far and found myself disappointed when I had finished.
I like the different characters and the special abilities.
I would love to see more levels and and endless mode in future. As it stands I can’t give it a full 5 stars as it does not yet represent value for money.

developer_response.body: Hey there! The game is now fully launched and updating will give you access to the full game with all three worlds and the endless mode. Let us know what you think!

reviews.review_description: It’s very aesthetically pleasing, very fun, great game just too easy.

developer_response.body: Hello! Thank you so much for taking the time to review! Have you tried jumping into the endless mode yet for a greater challenge?

reviews.review_title: Very fun. Additional idea also included
reviews.review_description:
Very fun game. You get hooked immediately that when you reach that last boss fight, you're bummed out. I love that there is an endless mode after completion.  And for the price it's 100% worth it. Can't wait to see what updates come next and what next game these devs have coming!

The only idea I recommend is tied to endless. If you can, please add an additional option for endless where it's the Confair belt option . That way, it's up to random chance what toy you get while trying to survive. Idk what the rules are with idea sharing, but definitely use this idea. I dont want credit. I just want to play it.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! Glad to hear you enjoy the game. Survival mode will be improved in the next update. Great idea to incorporate a conveyor belt mechanic. I will consider it.

reviews.review_description: Updated my review for full release, quite simply mind blowingly awesome. If you like plants Vs zombie before EA got there hands on it you will love this

Right now I am over level 100 in endless 3.
I think you can make it more difficult. Cus after level 50 they kinda feel the same. But over all it is a great vr game!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the five stars! We're monitoring for future difficulty balancing, thank you for the feedback 👌

reviews.review_title: The game that we didn't knew we NEEDED
reviews.review_description:
I never thought Plants vs. Zombies VR would become a reality, but here I am, sitting in my living room with a VR headset on and watching zombies being oliberated. As someone who grew up with pvz, I want to tell the developers that I am in love with their game. This game hits the sweet spot.

The game plays out similar to Plants vs. Zombies with it's own original spinoff. You defend your base against waves of evil. You use toys instead of plants, to defend against orcs instead of zombies.

Mixed reality is really well executed. You don't need a controller, but you still get a lot of feedback. For example, you can stun orcs with your own hands! The implementation of hand tracking & the functionality is excellent.

Levels get very challenging rather quickly, which is a good thing. 

Just a game done right. 11/10

Feature suggestions:
I'm not sure if it's already in the game. Something useful for removing misplaced toys/units (such as a shovel in pvz). It's a little annoying that I can't remove/replace a misplaced unit.

developer_response.body: Awesome! I'm glad to hear you're enjoying mixed reality so much! The topic of removing misplaced units has been a long-debated one within our team. While we haven't quite nailed it yet, we'll definitely revisit it for the next update

reviews.review_description:
An actually perfect game for mixed reality. 11/10 . 

 I wish I could pick my default hand power as switching between them is a bit tricky in the heat of the battle.

Please release DLCs with more levels and activities.
